about: Built in 1156-1157 by the Artuqid Ruler Fahrettin Karaaslan, the Harput Grand Mosque is one of Anatolia's most precious architectural treasures with its massive and thick-bodied brick minaret. Featuring an inclination of about 6.8 degrees, which is significantly greater than the famous Leaning Tower of Pisa in Italy (around 3.9 degrees), the fact that this minaret has remained standing at that angle under countless severe earthquakes is a miracle that confounds even modern science.
